ogTitle: Happy Valley vs Harcourt: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Happy Valley and Harcourt.
heading: Happy Valley vs Harcourt — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Happy Valley, Victoria and Harcourt, Victoria. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Harcourt has a lower overall climate risk score (31/100) compared to Happy Valley (35/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Happy Valley or Harcourt safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Happy Valley has a flood risk score of 53/100, while Harcourt scores 48/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Happy Valley scores 43/100 for bushfire risk. Harcourt scores 21/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Happy Valley or Harcourt?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Harcourt has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.8% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
